title: senecio cryptolanatus
intro: Senecio cryptolanatus, also known as Devil's Weed or Woolly Senecio, is a flowering plant native to Australia that has been used by the indigenous people for its medicinal properties. The plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been shown to have hepatotoxic effects and should be used with caution. However, when used in moderation, Senecio cryptolanatus has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. It can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its herbal benefits, although modern medical practitioners advise against its use due to potential side effects.
